He gives great examples of problems to make sure everyone understands. If you're looking for a strong basis in math, go take his courses!

I took the online course on single variable calculus and was so impressed with his teaching. All my life I've hated math but he made me fall in love with calculus, he made it appear so easy and intuitive. He is an exceptional professor.

Math 18.03 Differential Equations is a very beautiful class . The course use a lot of examples from Engineering, Physics, and other Sciences.  The Professor is great , he teach you to see beyond Differential Equations. The class is the most hard math requirement for an Engineering student, but is very interesting.

Math 18.01 is the first Calculus course at Massachusetts Institute of Technology. Covers All topics in Differentiation and Integration . The Professor explains well and Calculus is hard for so much people. Great Professor

Professor David Jerison at Massachussetts Institute of Technology is not the kind of Professor that you want to take Calculus. Math 18.01 Calculus I is pretty heavy , because covers Differentiation and Integration in one semester. You have to read Simmons Book and lecture notes are more worth it that going to class.

MIT Professor David Jerison for Math 18.01 is a bad choice. He explains very confusing with silly examples and very hard problems on the exam . You learn very few , the lectures notes and the book are better than the class . My friend Stacy take this class with other Professor and She learn so much that me in Fall 2006 .
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Makes everything more complicated than it should be.
good
Very good explanations and an obvious passion about Mathematics.
He is awesome

First time that i Take Math 18.03 Differential Equations was with Professor David Jerison. He explain The material but are enough simple in comparison of Its exams . You have to use The Book to understand.
His explanations aren't as clear as I expect them to be

He is a nice Proffessor very Clever but difficult to understand sometimes . Differential Equations ( Math 18.03 is difficult for much people. He Give difficult Exams , but explain a too Easy in the Class and that dont help
perfect prof for calculus  helped me so much   :)
Did not like him. Tried to make many awful jokes. Can be confusing.
Hated the way Jerison taught 18.03, difficult to follow lectures that weren't nearly as good as the MITx notes and unhelpful (and rather cold) when approached with questions.

I used his MIT OCW course to pass "The Filter" of my university, Calculus II. This professor is awesome and I wish I could take all calculus with him. I did have to work harder than usual to understand his class thoroughly,  then again, this is MIT's class.  As a bonus his jokes are very well timed. Everyone using OCW should be very thankful.
He's amazing professor. i've learned single variable calculus from him, via mit ocw, and it helped me   a lot

As a high school student with no background with calculus, but near-perfect averages in mathematics, this course seems easy for me, but he tries to make it a bit confusing, by joking sometimes. But it seems easy, obviously because of my skill level.

The professor seems like a pretty awesome, fun guy but I was really confused while taking his class. I would highly NOT recommend this class for anyone who hasn't had a Calculus background otherwise you will suffer in his class. I'm pretty sure for people with a calc background he's a good teacher to learn it with.
his lectures stir mathematical thinking.
i had some knowledge in calculus prior taking this course this guy made that very organised and i fell in love with calculus
I love this guy.
very good teacher
This is about his lectures on OCW  - teaches with clarity and makes everything look really simple. I love him!
he is brilliant
skipped many important points, incoherent explanations, few bright ideas.
This prof is amazing. For all the guys who said he wasn't you were not paying attention or perhaps you didn't understand. He teaches many intricacies that you won't find others taking up.
I don't know why this guy has bad reviews. I watched his lectures online for my Calculus I class, and he makes everything amazingly clear. It's as though he's a walking mathematics textbook.

(Based on the online course) Not sure how this professor ended up teaching at MIT. His lectures are very disorganized and he skips over many topics. I tried using this as a supplement to a Calculus course I was taking at a community college and found my CC professor to me much more thorough and clear. This guy is not the caliber of a Walter Lewin.

Check out the MIT OCW course "Calculus Revisited" for a masterfully prepared and well-thought-out presentation of most of this same material. I do not doubt his own personal math abilities as demonstrated in the lectures, but his teaching style almost assumes prior mastery of the subject. He unnecessarily complicates and obscures calculus' beauty.
I follow him on webcast. The best teacher I've seen. But, you have to pay attention. Everything he says is important.
He is so professional that it's a bliss to have him teach ppl.Such a extraordinary prof. P.S.: He is so cute
watching his lectures. really good. claryfies everything.
explains clearly enough for someone who just starts learning calculus, and tries very hard to explain every complicated part. He's a good and sincere professor leading freshes to calculus.
This prof teaches for people who understand math and can fill in the blanks themselves.  It is great for smart people, hard for other others, but then again MIT is for the smartest students.
Great

Watching him on OCW. Funny thing is I have taken Calc before and found it simple but if I were learning it for first time with this prof I would be so confused. He gives very little explanation of actual concepts. Gives examples of context but not concepts haha.
the best prof at MIT
The guy obviously knows his stuff and assumes everyone else in the room should find the material as easy as him. Lecturer yes, teacher no.
Thank you for the lectures in single variable calculus
Very good teacher. Watching him on OCW
Finally a math prof who doesn't write out every single step while working out the equations. For non-tech student might be hard to follow, for a EE it is a blast...
Very fast and clear in his thinking
He takes you round in circles.
His homework questions are not clear at times and at times is unreasonably harsh with the grading. Tries hard to explain things which ought to be easy.
He should be teaching at a community college, not MIT.

I just listened to his mathlecture on the MIT open courseware. After listening to his lectures I felt so stupid because I understood nothing of what he said. I understand how he goes from Step a to b to c and then to d, but then I lost the entire picture how a can go to d. I guess he can improve by focusing on elaborating on the whole picture more.
Realy bad
Awesome professor - explained everything really clearly and obviously knew what he was doing.  Used lots of helpful examples and connections, which made concepts a lot more understandable.
He was very helpful even though I didn't understand the material and had to drop the class.  I wish I had been able to keep up.
It's visible that he's trying very hard to teach us the material in a clear manner and does get better as he goes along.  A lot of potential to become a good professor.  Props for his efforts.
No Comments

Goes very quickly, and is not well organized. Fills up about 20 boards each lecture. Some days the lectures were much clearer. I think he is just early in his teaching career and still has some tricks to learn. Will likely improve with time. TONS of work.
Evil
Damned Sexy prof

A pretty decent professor, however, there are some issues when attempting to learn certain concepts. He uses 3 very standard examples for dynamic programming that do not build good intuition. I spent most of my time in the class just watching Tim Roughgarden's youtube videos. A genius professor, just slightly stuck-up.
was good all through the class
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I am a student who always ends up listening to his lectures when I start studying for my class quiz or when I have to do my assignment. Amazing lectures. Always awed by the way he drives the class.
ERIK DEMAINE IS FANTASTIC!  I want to be him when I grow up.  Fascinating research and a really engaging lecture.  I would take any of his classes, even if they're not in my department (18).

Her abuse and aggression are very verbal, tantrumy, and in the form of academic 'punishments' (removal from papers, projects and refusing to give recos). It is then followed by intermittent 'kindness' (again, only verbal and nothing tangible) to confuse you into staying working with her.

Yonina is completely indifferent to the harm she causes and the number of students she hurts along the way. I have often observed her taking perverse pleasure in sabotaging the careers of exceptionally talented students, or derailing their research journeys, purely for her own personal satisfaction and a callous disregard for others.
She is the best. Hands down.

Yonina shows some of the most hostile and toxic personality traits. But remember, (a) You're not alone. She has mistreated numerous students before. Institute is aware but does nothing about it, and (b) There's nothing wrong with you. Don't fall for her fabricated accusations. The problem lies with her, not you. Just get out of her clutches asap.
Awesome teacher!!! Very family-oriented. Love her classes! She understands that us students may need to prioritize our needs before her class. Very valuable lessons!

She is empathic only when it serves her. When she is nice, it is coz her interest is aligned with yours or she did not have something better to do that time. It is just that and really no more complicated than that. Otherwise, you cannot count on her for anything. When she comes thru, that is just luck. But luck is not how plan your career.

Thought it was a joke, but it's true. "Family-oriented" but divorced. LOL! After breaking homes of her mentees through her demanding nature, she finally broke her own home too. Now we'll hear sob-stories of trauma, villain ex, and heroic survival. Many bought into her wholesome image, but, in the lab, we always knew her marriage was just for show.

Brands herself as 'family-oriented', yet always pressured me to ignore my personal life for her pointless tasks. Now I hear she's divorced. Unsurprising! Credit to the man who endured years with someone so controlling, foul-mouthed, and intellectually average. She brings no value to research except her acerbic tongue and bad behavior.

Knew she was bad when I saw she had papers on nearly every topic in no time. Now, I see these reviews and it is clear that she is running a sweatshop and dicey collaborations to increase her paper count. Met her at a conference: she couldn't explain her own work, got irritated, and told me to ask her co-author instead. Total red flag.

Yonina just constantly lies. She demands endless labor from you with zero intention of rewarding or supporting you. Would offer fake praise to keep you compliant, but never real help. Ask for fairness or a recommendation, and she turns hostile, lashes out, silences you, and discards you like you're nothing. A predator in mentor's clothing. Avoid.

Tries to bait you by flashing her connections with Andrea Goldsmith and Al Oppenheim (who, for reasons unknown, are unable to see the charlatan she is). But these are only baits. She won't help or promote you—only mislead you. You'll end up worse off after joining her lab. Just look where her alumni end up—it's not MIT or Stanford.

She routinely launches into fabricated accusations out of thin air and pointing fingers at anyone but herself. Yet, the moment she's held to account for her own behavior or neglect of responsibilities, she erupts into fits of rage, shouting, bullying, and derailing the conversation. It's her go-to tactic: weaponize noise to dodge responsibility.

These reviews of Yonina are so spot on. Avoid her completely and don't join her lab in any capacity esp. as a PhD or postdoc. She is in a profession where she is supposed to nurture and mentor. But she only cares about herself and pretend that she cares about you. Just says nice things in public w/o meaning any of it. In private, she mistreats you.

She only cares about herself. That'll never change. Once you accept that, you can see that there is nothing normal, or kind, or safe about working with her. Takes a long time to realize that she is destructive to your career and mental health in so many different ways. So, do yourself a favor and leave her group or limit your contact with her asap.

Working with someone as calculating and self-serving as Yonina reveals a darker truth about academia: how seemingly trivial stakes, like a conference paper, a modest grant, or a hollow accolade, can drive individuals to ruthless extremes. In their pursuit of prestige and control, they're willing to ruin lives and careers of others without remorse.

Fanatically self-centered! Demands u prioritize her errands and career over ur own. The moment u need time for urself, she erupts in LOUD SCREAMS and unprofessional tantrums. Acts entitled, as if basic decency doesn't apply. Might be forgivable if she was a real scholar and not a fraud. When caught lying, runs away saying her kids need her - a LIE!

Yonina's pattern of misrepresentation starts from the very first meeting. She quickly makes grand claims, how her mentees go to Stanford or MIT. Fact: they do not. Most end up in industry or low-tier univs. Just check the list on her webpage yourself. Some even remain unemployed or stuck in postdoctoral positions even after 15 years.

She was my postdoc non-advisor in every sense: offered no guidance, saw me only as a paper-producing workhorse, ignored career concerns, and never lifted a finger to help, despite claiming Ivy League connections. Behind the fake smile lies shallow expertise and a pattern of taking credit for others' work. A complete failure as a mentor.
I am somewhat relieved to see that people are finally calling out Yonina's toxic behavior. Many thanks to RMP.

She is very good in manipulating people and drawing attention to her. But she is not able to hold up her half of a mentor-mentee relationship. I was her PhD student for 8 years. Published many papers. In the end, she refused to write me recommendation letters. She just wanted me to stay in her lab forever. That's the kind of selfish person she is.

Blames everyone and everything that her paper or projects are delayed/unfinished and that her students leave their degrees midway. Tries to play victim while seeking your help. No Yonina, you do not need any help. Your toxic behavior does a very good job of alienating anyone who works with you.

I worked with Yonina as my advisor 18 years ago. The trauma of her abusive behavior still haunts me. Given the multitude of previous bad experiences, I'm astonished she has any students or postdocs left. Story after story from her survivors highlights their bravery in sharing their experiences, revealing a consistent pattern of mistreatment.

If you want to see her true impact as researcher/advisor, look beyond her resume and consider the many students who have left her lab with unfinished degrees, incomplete projects, and without a job. If her mentees succeed, that has nothing to her credit. They succeed not because of her but in spite of her. All she does is to break their spirit.

Her staff, mentees, and collaborators often bear the brunt of her unpredictable behavior due to her bip0lar, as if it is their responsibility. When the effect of her med!cations fades, she becomes volatile and rageful. Difficult to rely on her as an advisor. When it will come for her to return the favors, she would act as if you are a non-entity.

Easily the most destructive PhD advisor in the institute, and possibly the entire field. No real research. Her sub-Nyquist theory is industry deadweight. In 25+ years, barely 10 PhDs solely under her. Most fled with an MS to escape her toxicity and flawed research.

She smiles only when she needs something from you. Behind closed doors, she lashes out at students and staff alike, berating, belittling, and breaking spirits with impunity. Do not mistake her manipulation for mentorship.

Screams, insults, intimidates, punishes academically, and even outright harasses to manipulate you into giving her whatever trivial thing she desires. It could be something as small as one slide for her presentation, yet she can't help but create a scene to get them. Once you comply, she switches to a fake sweet demeanor, until her next outburst.

Absolutely terrible at teaching. Learned nothing ever. Also has a terrible attitude and screams at EVERYONE all the TIME!!! The first thing I think of entering her class is when it ends. Truly the worst hours of my life were spent in this classroom.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: She's like that insurance agent who promises you the stars. After you sign up for their scheme, they go silent, phone off, untraceable. When you realize you've been played, they've already moved on to the next gullible target. Turns out, they weren't even a real agent, just a smooth-talker with flashy connections, managed awards, and a sales pitch!

Her publication record may suggest that she's mentored dozens of PhDs. But, in 25 years, some ~10 PhD have graduated under her sole guidance. Most students leave midway or switch to a master's degree. Her CV may impress, but it offers little to her mentees.

She sees everyone as mere tools to be exploited, never as individuals to be nurtured. To her, students are rivals to be outpaced, not mentored. Attempt to engage her in a deep technical discussion, and she'll feign annoyance, shutting you down, because beneath her facade, she lacks the technical understanding to keep up.

You can't believe a word Yonina says. Her lies are so relentless and insidious that every sentence she utters demands scrutiny. Don't waste your time engaging with her or her followers. My advice: Cut ties, walk away, and don't look back. Leave her lab before it consumes you. Some battles aren't worth fighting; escape while you still can.

Yonina exhibits severe emotional dysregulation, frequently erupting into intense, uncontrollable fits of anger and screaming. Attempting to speak out against her behavior only exacerbates the situation. Fails to articulate any constructive needs or guidance. Instead, she displays entitlement, expecting you to blindly comply with arbitrary demands.

Issues with Yonina have nothing to do with research. Her students and collaborators face something very specific: She is pathologically selfish to the highest degree. Everything is about her. She will not attune to a conversation with you unless it's of some interest to her. However, she would not reciprocate any favors or efforts you pull for her.

She shows no guilt or remorse, with people constantly leaving her lab due to her toxic behavior. Relationships quickly sour after joining, as her behavior shifts dramatically and deliberately. She can't handle being questioned, often erupting in rage every few weeks. This is not how true achievers act. She is a very insecure person.

Meetings with Yonina are often unproductive. Despite her saying, "I'm happy to discuss," she doesn't genuinely listen to your concerns. The conversation never leads to a resolution. Yet, she will often gaslight you that the matter was addressed, asking, "We discussed this, right?" when no progress was actually made. Very n@rc!ss!stic behavior!

She pretends to care, but rarely addresses your concerns. She believes that her problems are the entire lab's responsibility, yet the issues of lab members are not hers to deal with. When you need career help, you're on your own. Yet if she needs a favor, you're expected to help her without question. Of course, most lab members quit midway.

With Yonina, everything is ME, ME, ME. She wants you to focus on her needs and not on your own. Thinks that bullying and interrupting others is the way to go. Does not do any research. Just gets her name on papers. You cannot get any original idea from her. She borrows/steals every single idea from her students or collaborators behind their back.

Yonina's self-centered approach toward advising makes it evident that your growth or success is not a priority for her, though it is literally her job to support her advisees. In contrast, she only wants her students to support her quest of awards and fame. Once she has finished exploiting you, she discards you and swiftly moves on to next target.

Do not join her lab esp. if you are foreign student/postdoc. She has very supr3mac!st views and will discriminate against you. She recommends only students from her ethnicity/faith to good universities and opportunities.

Her greed and exploitation of grad students, postdocs, and virtually everyone around her are truly boundless. Despite completing 8 journal papers, she insisted I produce 20 more to graduate. Provided no meaningful input to research. Our meetings revolved around her personal issues or unrealistic demands for even more papers.

Terrible person. Uses gender card when convenient. Mostly hires men in her lab and subjects them to all kinds of harassment, while relying on them for most of her research output. Despite her public persona, she has never produced any successful female academic. Views them as her competitors. She herself was advised by a male prof.

Lacks conscience and empathy. Traps students and engineers in her lab by making big and fake promises to improve their careers. Her toxic behavior starts quickly, within days or weeks of joining her lab. When you resist her unreasonable demands for clerical work (not even research!), she SCREAMS publicly and sends threatening emails.
Her typical response to being held accountable is, 'I don't remember' or 'that never happened,' deflecting as if her memory loss is somehow your responsibility.

She's quick to criticize others but can't seem to handle any criticism herself. This kind of behavior reflects a classic inability to take accountability. Given the multitude of previous bad experiences, I'm astonished she has any students/postdocs left. Story after story from her survivors reveals a consistent pattern of mistreatment.

She often dangles false promises of opportunities at prestigious institutions like MIT and Stanford to coerce students into completing projects. Once the project is done, she would renege on her promises, demanding even more work. Does not take responsibility for anything and will pin everything on someone else.

Genuinely rude and unhelpful. Behaves more like a princess, diva or woman-child, making people around her miserable wherever she goes. Says nice things but never means or acts on them. Merely takes credit for the work of others. Never supports her mentees in their careers but wants them to perpetually work for her.

Lacks originality in research and tends to rely on others, especially early-career researchers, for new ideas. Often delegates these ideas to collaborators to produce similar papers, subsequently taking credit for their work. Attempts to engage in deep technical discussions with her are often met with uncooperative behavior or unnecessary drama.

I was Yonina's grad student until recently. She is mean mean mean. Hardly any meeting went by when she did not project her toxicity on me—screaming, threats to cut funding, firing, and degrading comments like, "You're useless" or "You'll never succeed." She's unstable and unfit to mentor any student or postdoc. Think twice before working with her.

Yonina treats people as tools to advance her personal ambitions, with little regard for reciprocity. She expects others to prioritize her issues while remaining indifferent to theirs. Be warned: if you're co-authoring a paper with her, it's likely being duplicated in collaboration with someone else behind your back.

Collaborated with her on a paper. Experience could not have been worse, esp. when I noticed she took my ideas for the follow-up papers to other collaborators. Conscienceless ppl like her get more praise, have more enablers, and hence, are more emboldened to harm you. Yonina falls in this category.

Yonina's promises are worthless; she will backtrack at the first opportunity. She would often dangle false promises of opportunities at prestigious institutions like MIT and Stanford to coerce students into completing projects. Once the project was done, she would renege on her promises, demanding even more work.

Never meet her alone. She will bully you, not letting you speak, interrupt you frequently. When you protest, she throws tantrums, screams, runs away, slams doors. This kind of entitlement is typical of people who have moved forward in their career by kissing *** of their advisers like Al Oppenheim.
So hard to believe that someone as incompetent like her could be associated with MIT. She takes ideas from others and then gets collaborators to write papers on them.

Yonina thinks she can BS her way to fool all students and lure them to join her lab or get them to write a paper on which she can put her name. You will not learn anything from her. She only has superficial knowledge and tries to skip details or delegate them to someone else - and its true, do not expect her for any help in your career.
If you are not from her native country, do not collaborate with her or join her lab. She has very supremacist views and will actively discriminate against you.

Yonina was complained about so much in Technion that the institute decided to go with the age-old "pass the harasser" solution. They just let her to be hired by Weizmann so that she can stop terrorizing the engineers, staff, colleagues, and students in Technion. I'm sure many in the department were relieved to see her leave.

True that she is a full-time prof in Israel. But legit to review her here because she is a visiting scholar in MIT and inflicts misery on everyone who comes in her contact and everywhere she goes. She loses temper over small things—would scream, yell, curse in group meetings. Her language and behavior aren't constructive at all.

I found interactions with Yonina surprisingly empty and utterly devoid of genuine connection. She constantly evaluates you based on how useful you are to her agenda. Once you've fulfilled your purpose or if you express inability to meet her demands, her interest in you plummets immediately. Her behavior is very transactional and manipulative.

Did my thesis with her. Lab environment was toxic and dysfunctional, perpetually on the brink of implosion due to her anger management issues. Interactions with her were characterized by relentless shouting, particularly during group meetings and phone calls. When frustrated, she would SCREAM, that could be heard a few blocks down the hallway.

If you are interested in foreign policy, his lectures are fantastic. If not, you may want to find another CI-H. He is always willing to stick around after class for discussion and has the most fascinating stories. He is one of the most knowledgable and well-read people I have ever met.
Not the best. Class was pretty boring. Give 17.40 a miss.

He is really boring.  He focuses on the most mundane details.  The articles he assigns could put an insomniac to sleep. He makes me never want to take political science again, and I was extremely interested in it before. Don't take it.
LOVED his classes, interesting, and so clear.
Awesome
class was semi-boring.  he has high respect for students and does a good job teaching, interesting topics, pretty good class, graded fairly
No Comments

Enectali Figueroa Feliciano was a great teacher In physics. He looks like Marc Anthony teaching Physics. He is from Puerto Rico . He studied at Stanford University. He is an expert in Dark Matter and Astrophysics in General. He challenges you . But is not the greatest at Massachusetts Institute of Technology.

Physics at Massachusetts Institute of Technology with Professor Enectali Feliciano was a great Professor . He is an expert of Astrophysics and comes from Puerto Rico. He is an excellent lecturer the exams are a little tricky. He is at Norwestern University now.
He is a nice professor but he is also a TOUGH one.
Franz has harsh requirements for grading but he's such a fantastic lecturer that it works out. Single-handedly the reason I liked this class as much as I did.
Please do not take a mechanical engineering class if it is taught by Franz. It will be much harder than when it is taught by a different instructor (he said so himself). 89.5 is a B, 79.5 is a C.
Hover is very knowledgeable of the subject material. TAs are not as good at tying p-sets to material.
No Comments
Amazing Professor! His diverse knowledge and depth of biology dazzles everyone.
He is THE BEST professor to ever step foot in the universe!
He is such a professor. He's so intelligent, yet so humble! I love Eric Lnader
He's just such a great, humble,amazing (Don't forget Extreme sense of humor). He's the best teacher I have ever met!
hard
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Here's the deal - I hate biology. I've tried to enjoy it, it's just my least favorite subject. HOWEVER, Eric Lander somehow made dragging myself out of bed to go to a lecture on my least favorite subject worth it. God bless you, Professor Lander
Excellent Professor of Biology so fun.  I studied NSE biology is very useful and easy

If i have The opportunity take Bio 7.012 First Semester Biology at MIT . He is The Best Professor a genius really. He Explain genetics, Microbiology, Biochemistry and other Topics like different Ecosystems all in one Class . Massachussetts Institute of Technology is The best University and Its shown by The Quality of Its Professors

Bio 7.012 is Fundamentals of Biology at Massachussetts Institute of Technology. Its lectures are amazing. If you read and Study you Pass with an A. He is The Best Professor of Biology and explain Genetics like no One can. Definitely Take you learn so much and he is very intelectual. Biology was The Best course at The First year.

Clear, exciting, inspiring, thought provoking and riveting lectures. Difficult concepts for many. The software employed is helpful for understanding the concepts. Eric introduces concepts as things to be worked out. You think along with scientific development. Genomics - emphasized.
Great course for catching up with what's been happening in molecular genetics over the 45 years since I first studied it.
The best Biology professor!
My favorite teacher so far! His ability to draw you in the world of biology, the way he teaches, his dedication are second to none!

Prof. Lander is an expert in his field and a gifted lecturer. He makes very complex material not only comprehensible but enjoyable to learn. His enthusiasm in the subject matter is contagious. Lastly, he has a terrific sense of humor.
The most inspiring. Lecturer I have ever had.  A real treat to have him lecture.
He's by far the best professor I have ever studied under. I was quite skeptical about biology at first, but it's now become one of the subjects I'm most interested in, thanks to his class.
BEST of the Best
A truly exceptional professor. His lectures are thorough, enthralling and thought provoking.
MOST AMAZING PROF EVER
You'll be satisfied ... believe me ...
Eric Lander is a world renowned biologist who worked on the human genome projects. He alternates lectures with Weinberg, who is very smart but is a terrible lecturer.
He's an amazing professor. He's very engaging and obviously passionate about what he does. I'm sad that the semester is already halfway over!

If I had to describe Professor Lander in one word, it would be simply: PHENOMENAL!  His passion for the subject matter is blatantly apparent (making it extremely interesting), the time flew by, and I wish that more teachers were like him.  I also wish that he would teach EVERY class at MIT!!!!  Much love! Stay teaching FOREVER!!!!
An amazing person to speak with! Like an avuncular relative, but brilliant :)

Eric Lander is the most organized professor I have ever had - he always uses all 9 chalkboards, and uses them all.  His lectures end on time, involve little mysteries, and are crystal clear.  Sadly I didn't learn anything at his lectures...
Clear Lectures....Graded fairly easily....
Great professor
one of the top 5 lecturers in the school
yeah
Excellent, well structured lectures
No Comments
No Comments
During the lecture she only reads slides, without explaining much. The test wasn't too hard, but she is very scrict in grading.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment:
She fumbles and mumbles in nearly all of her sentences while delivering a lecture, making it extremely difficult to focus and follow along. The concept notes are rather jargon-heavy, and examples hardly convey the underlying concept clearly. 

It is nearly painful to follow along in her class, luckily it was 14.310x and online.

Probably a genius in her field, but somewhat pedagogically challenged. She reads off her slides, uses incredibly technical language, does not read the room, and still makes very many mistakes. In addition, examples are unnecessarily complex and confuse rather than illustrate concepts.
Hard to follow, Psets inconsistent with lectures

Frequent misstatements, mistakes and missing content in notes, instructions to recall from several lectures back without explanation, and inadequate detail in explanatory math. The few examples focus on testing calculus & notation expertise rather than building intuition. Little integration of concepts. Books full of errors and ambiguity.

I think it is very inspirational that a woman is teaching 14.01 at MIT.  It would be even more inspirational if two women in charge of the subject.  However, Ellison shouldn't teach any lectures during 2023 unless the scandal blows over.  Students will be thinking, what's going on with Caroline this week?  Need to invite a visiting professor again.

Very nice but can't teach. She makes a lot of mistakes and seems nervous. When she's solving a problem she often has to back peddle because she made a mistake, which leads to a lot of confusion. Biggest critique is that she doesn't show her work for a lot of the problems she works through. She does a lot of solving in her head... sorry not sorry
Most famous theoretical physicist there is really

Incredible Teacher,there he was teaching us Physics 20 years ago and today he just removed the Combine from Earth and re-enabled the internet Now we are a free race :) Would totally recommend this professor if your planet is currently being overrun by an alien species but I would ask the G-Man for permission first, otherwise just for academic help.
